title: Boston sues social media companies over ‘addictive’ features, joining nationwide litigation
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston Mayor Michelle Wu announced a lawsuit against Meta, TikTok, Snapchat, and YouTube for allegedly targeting minors with addictive features.
The lawsuit, representing Boston Public Schools, seeks to remove addictive features and compensate for student mental health support.
Data shows a rise in mental health issues among Boston high school students, prompting expansion of support staff in schools.

title: Cyclospora, the explosive diarrhea parasite, has reached Mass. Here’s what to know.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Massachusetts reports 18 cases of cyclosporiasis between May 1 and July 7, aligning with typical numbers for this period.
The CDC tracked 145 nationwide cyclosporiasis cases by June 16, excluding Michigan's significant outbreak with nearly 800 cases.
Cyclospora symptoms include prolonged diarrhea and cramping; spread is through contaminated food or water, treatable with antibiotics.

title: Jaylen Brown says he and Patriots WR A.J. Brown are cousins
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown of the Celtics and A.J. Brown of the Patriots are cousins, sharing a grandfather relationship.
Jaylen Brown was traded to the 76ers, while A.J. Brown joined the Patriots from the Eagles this summer.
Both Browns won titles in 2024; Jaylen with the Celtics and A.J. with the Eagles in the Super Bowl.

title: Carles Gil selected for 2026 MLS All-Star roster
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Carles Gil, 33, was selected to the 2026 MLS All-Star roster as the only representative of New England Revolution.
The MLS All-Star Game against Mexico's LIGA MX will be held at Charlotte's Bank of America Stadium on July 29.
Gil's selection marks his fifth All-Star appearance; he has five goals and four assists in 14 games this season.

title: Massachusetts man who fell ill in Fiji dies
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Scott Winslow, a Holbrook resident, died in Fiji after falling critically ill from an infection during a sailing trip.
Winslow's condition deteriorated rapidly, leading to admission in a local hospital's intensive care unit.
Attempts to medflight Winslow to Mass General Hospital were denied; his daughters traveled to Fiji to say goodbye.

title: The Brigham and Women’s Hospital nurses strike is underway. Here’s what to know.
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Over 4,000 nurses began a strike at Brigham and Women's Hospital in Boston on July 8, 2026.
The strike, led by the Massachusetts Nurses Association, centers on disputes over pay increases and workplace protections.
Brigham and Women's Hospital continues operations with replacement nurses, advising extra travel time for appointments.

title: 2 of the most charming main streets in America are in New England, according to Travel + Leisure
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Nantucket, Massachusetts and Brunswick, Maine were ranked among the 10 most charming main streets in the U.S. by Travel + Leisure.
Nantucket's Main Street is praised for its Greek Revival homes and historic charm resembling the 1800s.
Brunswick's Maine Street is noted for its small-town New England charm with boutiques and a well-loved 1965 doughnut shop.

title: ‘The Sixers just got away with murder’: Charles Barkley thought the Jaylen Brown trade was fake news
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Charles Barkley was surprised by the Celtics' decision to trade Jaylen Brown to the 76ers.
The 76ers acquired Jaylen Brown in exchange for Paul George and draft picks.
Barkley believes the trade makes the 76ers contenders while criticizing the Celtics' strategy.

title: Watch: Drake Maye dials up Noah Kahan at Fenway, as Gigi Perez joins in for ‘She Calls Me Back’
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Drake Maye surprised fans with a video cameo during Noah Kahan's concert at Fenway Park on Tuesday night.
Kahan picked up a ringing pay phone on stage, humorously pretending to answer a call from rapper Drake.
Gigi Perez joined Noah Kahan on stage to perform 'She Calls Me Back,' exciting the Fenway Park audience.

title: Here’s who won the rain jackpot for Mass. on Monday and Tuesday
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Falmouth recorded the highest rainfall in Massachusetts on July 6-7, 2026, with 5.42 inches.
Woods Hole was the second highest with 5.04 inches, leading to some minor flooding.
Rainfall amounts dropped significantly outside these areas, with Oak Bluffs receiving 2.92 inches and Nantucket 2.20 inches.
